title: The Venezuela Earthquakes Hit a Health System Already in Crisis
summary: Firefighters are using cellphone lights because of a flashlight shortage, and an overwhelmed hospital in the disaster zone is operating without running water, one doctor said.

title: Trump Says Iran Attack on Cargo Ship Violated the Cease-fire
summary: The president did not say how or whether he would respond, a day after Iran fired on a container ship that was transiting the Strait of Hormuz, an act Mr. Trump called “foolish.”

title: Oil Prices Return to Prewar Levels After Four Months
summary: The cost of crude has become a real-time barometer of the Iran war’s toll on the global economy.

title: Americans Are Feeling Better About the Economy, Consumer Survey Shows
summary: Consumer sentiment rose in recent weeks after plummeting early this year as energy costs soared.

title: Pro-Palestinian Activists Sense a Tide Turning After N.Y. Primary Wins
summary: After years of operating on the fringe of Democratic values, pro-Palestinian activists felt validated after the primary wins by several candidates who oppose Israel’s actions.

title: Ukrainian Attacks Spur State of Emergency Declaration in Crimea
summary: Weeks of intense strikes by Ukraine have rattled everyday life in Crimea to an extent unseen since Russia illegally annexed the peninsula in 2014.

title: Zelensky Steps Up Threats Against Belarus for Aiding Drone Attacks
summary: Ukrainian officials say their northern neighbor is allowing its radio relay stations to be used to guide Russian attack drones more precisely.

title: John Bolton, Former Trump Adviser, Pleads Guilty in Classified Information Case
summary: Mr. Bolton admitted to mishandling classified information and could face time in prison, in an inquiry that spanned the Trump and Biden administrations.

title: How Trump’s Political Agenda Is Shaped by His Own Obsessions
summary: President Trump’s priorities seem increasingly detached from the concerns of voters and his party.

title: Vance Downplays Watergate and Compares Himself to Nixon
summary: The vice president said that the scandal that ended Richard Nixon’s presidency would be “like a 12-hour news story” if it happened today and that the “deep state” had taken down Nixon.

title: Yale Seeks Trump Administration Deal in Inquiry Over Race and Admissions
summary: The university hired a high-powered law firm to try to reach an agreement with the Justice Department over claims its admissions practices hurt white and Asian applicants.

title: Freedom Con: Thousands of Men Gather for Christianity and Feats of Strength
summary: Thousands of men and boys gathered in central Washington for masculinity, Christianity and right-wing politics.

title: With ‘Supergirl,’ Milly Alcock is Learning to Accept the Fear
summary: After “House of the Dragon,” a starring role in another big franchise, “Supergirl,” gave Milly Alcock pause. It’s “this new gift of learning to accept the fear.”

title: ‘Supergirl’ Review: This Glass Ceiling Is Made of Kryptonite
summary: Milly Alcock is naturally appealing in this derivative, if altogether watchable, superhero movie.

title: The Man Who Cried Goooooooooooal
summary: The Telemundo announcer Andrés Cantor must train to deliver his famed scoring celebration for the World Cup, where he could call 20 games in about a month.

title: Best Investments Over the Last 100 Years? Almost All Are Tech Companies.
summary: What’s most surprising is that Tesla and SpaceX have entered that elite group. A vast majority of companies weren’t worth owning, a long-running study shows.

title: In Europe, Tesla Sales Are Rising Despite Views on Elon Musk
summary: Price cuts and low-interest-rate loans are luring buyers, including people offended by the company’s chief executive.

title: Small Plane Crashes Into Tallest Building in Beijing
summary: Crowds in the capital’s central business district fled as the crash sent debris falling into the streets below.

title: In London, You Can Celebrate America’s 250th Birthday From the British Side
summary: Britain may have lost the Revolutionary War, but London still holds the blueprints of the American Revolution. Follow in the founders’ footsteps at these notable places.

title: For the U.S.’s 250th Birthday, a German Declaration of Independence Goes on Show
summary: The presentation of an original German-language translation in Berlin is an opportunity to consider the shared history of the United States and Germany.

title: Why Is the Supreme Court Unshackling the Presidency?
summary: Where is Chief Justice John Roberts? And why isn’t he putting limits on this administration?

title: In Cuba, Where So Many Have Left, These Artists Have Stayed
summary: Four young Cubans grapple with what it means to stay on an island facing unprecedented migration.

title: Canada’s 24 Sussex Drive Is a Dump. Mark Carney Is Going to Fix It Up.
summary: The prime minister’s official residence in Ottawa, in disrepair and vacant for more than a decade, has become the country’s most prominent home renovation project.
